name: Vikas Varma Pokala
summary:
Software Engineer with 2+ years of experience building production-grade backend and full stack applications across startup and enterprise environments.
Hands-on experience across Java, Go, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, SQL, Spring Boot, Node.js, React, REST, GraphQL, microservices, and cloud-based application workflows.
Strong background in backend services, API integrations, database-driven systems, secure authentication/authorization, and scalable service-oriented design.
Also contributed to selected AI-enabled product features, including transcription, workflow automation, retrieval-based assistant capabilities, and private-data integrations.
Maintained a broader engineering focus across full stack systems while delivering reliable product and platform improvements.
skills: Languages: Java, Go, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, SQL
Frontend: React.js, Redux, React Router, React Query / RTK, HTML5, CSS3 / LESS, Bootstrap, Axios, AJAX
Backend: Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, Hibernate / JPA, Java EE, Node.js, Express.js, JAX-RS, REST APIs, GraphQL, Socket.IO, Microservices
Databases: MySQL, Oracle, MongoDB, Firebase Firestore
Cloud / DevOps: AWS, S3, Lambda, KMS, Transcribe, CloudWatch, Docker, Jenkins, Git, Maven, Gradle, CI/CD
Security: Spring Security, JWT, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, RBAC, scoped access control, API key lifecycle management
Testing / Tools: JUnit, Jest, Mocha, Chai, Cucumber, Selenium, Postman, GraphQL Playground
AI / LLM Integrations: OpenAI, Gemini, Whisper, Pinecone, RAG workflows, private-data retrieval
Architecture / Practices: Microservices, service-oriented architecture, clean architecture, SOLID principles, event-driven systems, webhook integrations, Agile / Scrum, code reviews, debugging

experience.description:
Built production-grade backend and full stack features across Java/Spring Boot services, React applications, and integration layers for healthtech and enterprise products.
Developed APIs, business logic, and service workflows using Java, Go, Node.js, Python, and SQL to support product features, internal tools, and third-party integrations.
Designed and maintained REST and GraphQL endpoints for frontend applications, middleware-driven request flows, and secure service-to-service communication.
Worked across distributed microservices with a strong focus on service boundaries, integration design, maintainability, and scalable backend architecture.
Applied Spring Boot, Spring Cloud, Hibernate/JPA, and SQL-based access patterns to support transactional workflows and backend service development.
Built and enhanced React-based user interfaces for operational and healthcare workflows using reusable components and structured client-side state patterns.
Integrated third-party APIs and webhook-based services with controlled request handling, resilient event processing, and secure data exchange patterns.
Worked with MySQL, Oracle, MongoDB, and Firebase Firestore to support transactional systems, real-time application state, and backend data workflows.
Implemented authentication and authorization flows using JWT, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, RBAC, and scoped access controls across frontend and backend systems.
Used AWS services including S3, Lambda, KMS, Transcribe, and CloudWatch, along with Docker and CI/CD workflows, to support backend processing, monitoring, and secure storage.
Contributed to unit testing, integration testing, debugging, and code reviews in Agile/Scrum teams to improve reliability and release quality.
Led end-to-end design and implementation of the AI-assisted clinical note generation workflow for CliniScripts using Spring Boot backends and LLM workflow design, reducing average note generation time from ~60s to under 30s and improving clinician efficiency.
Architected and implemented the speech-to-text/transcription pipeline (provider routing, streaming logic, fallback handling) leveraging AWS Transcribe and custom streaming logic, cutting transcription cost from ~$1.00/hour to ~$0.07/hour while increasing production reliability.
Built the private-data copilot (RAG) workflow end-to-end: implemented file upload, embedding storage in Pinecone, and retrieval-based context injection for grounded assistant responses, enabling secure user-specific document Q&A and higher response relevance.
Optimized production workflows and monitoring (CloudWatch, Docker, Jenkins CI/CD) for these AI features, owning deployment tasks and validating improvements via production metrics and cost tracking.

projects.name: CliniScripts
projects.description:
Contributed to an AI-powered clinical documentation platform focused on secure transcription, structured note generation, and clinician-facing workflows.
Built backend and AI-assisted workflow features that improved note-generation speed by 50% through workflow optimization, request handling, and LLM workflow design.
Reduced transcription costs by 70% by improving provider usage strategy, streaming logic, and fallback handling across speech-to-text workflows.
Implemented request-control mechanisms for AI-powered endpoints to improve platform stability and controlled usage.
Developed a private-data copilot capability that enabled file upload, embedding storage in Pinecone, and retrieval of relevant context for grounded responses.
Supported retrieval-based assistant behavior using vector search, LLM workflows, and user-specific context injection in secure healthcare-oriented environments.
Implemented secure authentication, scoped authorization, and access-control patterns for backend endpoints using AWS KMS and S3 for encrypted storage, improving safeguarded data exchange and reducing risk of unauthorized access in clinician workflows.
Built and owned backend endpoints and middleware for transcription and embeddings flows, integrating AWS Transcribe for streaming speech-to-text, OpenAI for LLM workflow design, and Pinecone SDK for vector storage to support private-data retrieval and grounded assistant responses.
Optimized request-control mechanisms (production middleware and API gateways) that contributed to measured note-generation speed improvements (~50%) and transcription-cost reductions (~70%) by shaping request patterns, fallback handling, and streaming logic.
Instrumented production monitoring and logging with AWS CloudWatch and structured telemetry to improve reliability and observability of AI-powered endpoints, enabling faster incident response and stable clinician-facing documentation workflows.

projects.name: TELUS Marketplace
projects.description:
Contributed to backend and middleware development for TELUS marketplace systems supporting IoT connectivity, SIM-related flows, ordering, and device-management operations.
Worked across Java-based microservices and enterprise backend components to support scalable business logic, service-to-service communication, and transaction-heavy workflows.
Built GraphQL and Go-based middleware services between frontend applications and backend systems, handling integration flows, response shaping, and API coordination.
Supported API contracts, distributed integration patterns, debugging, and testing to help deliver stable backend capabilities in an enterprise environment.
Implemented and hardened Go-based middleware handlers and GraphQL resolvers to improve integration stability between frontend apps and Java backend services, contributing directly to production readiness.
Authored and validated API contracts and integration tests using existing test frameworks to reduce integration defects during deployments and simplify debugging for the team.
Provided hands-on debugging across Java microservices and Go/GraphQL middleware, improving service maintainability and operational readiness through targeted fixes and triage.
